name: Allied Cash Advance
description_short: At NE Corner US Highway, 264 Indian Rte 12 Unit #6, Window Rock, AZ 86515, Allied Cash Advance can be reached at 1-928-482-2468.
description_long: Allied Cash Advance is a brick-and-mortar lending company operating physical store locations, including one in Mesa, Arizona at 2706 East University Drive. The company has been established long enough to maintain a customer base and operational infrastructure, though specific founding details are not provided on their website. Their primary business model centers on providing short-term lending solutions to customers who need rapid access to cash, primarily through in-store applications and consultations. The company maintains extended operating hours (10 AM–6 PM weekdays, 10 AM–2 PM Saturday) to accommodate working customers. Allied Cash Advance offers installment loans, which are unsecured personal loans repaid on a fixed schedule, intended for major purchases, bill consolidation, or unexpected expenses. They also provide Netspend Prepaid Mastercard products and retail remote refinancing/reloading options for existing customers. Loan decisions are made through their underwriting process, which considers credit history but does not explicitly require good credit. Funding timelines are competitive: same-day funding available for in-store applicants using debit cards (as quickly as 30 minutes), and next-business-day funding for online applicants. The Mesa location maintains a strong 4.9-star Google rating with 377 reviews, with customer feedback specifically praising staff members by name for professionalism and patience. The company requires standard documentation including proof of income, valid government-issued ID, proof of active checking account (30+ days), and in Arizona, proof of vehicle ownership. While the website contains a maintenance notice, it provides comprehensive information about loan products, application requirements, and customer testimonials. The main limitation is that specific APR ranges, fee structures, and loan amount limits are not disclosed on the website, making it difficult for consumers to compare total costs upfront.

pros: Same-day funding available for in-store applicants using debit cards (as fast as 30 minutes)
No explicit credit score requirement—company considers applicants with bad or no credit
Convenient physical location with extended hours (10 AM–6 PM weekdays, Saturday morning availability)
High customer satisfaction: 4.9-star Google rating with 377 verified reviews
Quick application process described as taking minutes to complete
Staff praised by name in reviews for professionalism, patience, and helpfulness
Multiple application methods: online, in-store, and remote reloans for existing customers
cons: APR rates, fee structures, and maximum loan amounts are not disclosed on the website
Unsecured installment loans typically carry higher interest rates than traditional bank loans
Limited to physical store locations—no indication of mobile lending or expanded geographic availability
Website was down for 'system maintenance' at time of review, suggesting potential technical reliability concerns
No mention of credit-building benefits or financial counseling services despite short-term lending focus
best_for: Consumers who need cash within hours and can visit a physical location in person
Borrowers with poor or no credit history who have been rejected by traditional banks
Arizona residents needing vehicle-based lending with proof of ownership
Existing customers looking to refinance or reload current installment loans
diagnosis: Allied Cash Advance is best for borrowers in Arizona who need emergency cash within hours and have access to a physical store location, particularly those with poor credit or no credit history. The major caveat is that the website does not disclose APR rates, fees, or maximum loan amounts, making it impossible to assess the true cost of borrowing before applying—consumers must contact the store directly or apply to receive pricing details.
services: Installment loans (unsecured personal loans with fixed repayment schedules)

Netspend Prepaid Mastercard (reloadable debit card alternative)
Retail remote refinancing for existing customers
